correlation_steps_to_scripts.test_step: Implicit Step – Required for Test Execution - Setup and navigation to shortcuts screen (Steps 1-4: Install and Launch the HPX app, Click on the printer icon on the root view screen, Then click on the shortcuts tile on the device details page, Click on Add new shortcuts in shortcuts screen)
correlation_steps_to_scripts.mapped_script_line: Prerequisite steps executed before test function starts

correlation_steps_to_scripts.test_step: Step 5: Then click on the Create your own shortcut arrow button
Step 6: Click on the back button in Add Shortcut screen
Step 7: Verify the pop up screen - Expected Result: Cancel this Shortcut?' pop up window should be displayed
correlation_steps_to_scripts.mapped_script_line: Implicit Step – Supporting Step – Purpose of Test Case Validation - Verification is handled by the click_cancel_shortcut_go_back_btn() method or subsequent assertion not shown in this function

intermediary_steps.test_script_step: Implicit Step – Required for Test Execution - Setup and navigation to shortcuts screen (Steps 1-4: Install and Launch the HPX app, Click on the printer icon on the root view screen, Then click on the shortcuts tile on the device details page, Click on Add new shortcuts in shortcuts screen)
intermediary_steps.automation_script_step: Prerequisite steps executed before test function starts
steps: Initialize test environment and device connection
Install HPX application on target test device
Launch HPX application and wait for home screen load
Locate printer icon element on root view screen using resource-id or content-desc locator
Execute click action on printer icon to navigate to device details
Identify shortcuts tile element on device details page
Perform click action on shortcuts tile to access shortcuts screen
Locate 'Add new shortcuts' option on shortcuts screen
Click on 'Add new shortcuts' to prepare for shortcut creation workflow

intermediary_steps.test_script_step: Step 5: Then click on the Create your own shortcut arrow button
steps: Locate 'Create your own shortcut' arrow button element using resource-id or xpath locator
Verify button element is displayed and enabled for interaction
Execute click action on the arrow button element
Wait for navigation transition to Add Shortcut screen
Validate successful screen transition to shortcut creation interface

intermediary_steps.test_script_step: Step 6: Click on the back button in Add Shortcut screen
steps: Identify back/cancel button element in Add Shortcut screen header
Verify button element is visible and clickable
Execute click action on back/cancel button
Monitor for UI state change or popup trigger
Capture action result for subsequent validation

intermediary_steps.test_script_step: Step 7: Verify the pop up screen - Expected Result: Cancel this Shortcut?' pop up window should be displayed
intermediary_steps.automation_script_step: Implicit Step – Supporting Step – Purpose of Test Case Validation - Verification is handled by the click_cancel_shortcut_go_back_btn() method or subsequent assertion not shown in this function
steps: Wait for popup dialog element to appear after back button click
Locate popup container using dialog resource-id or alert locator
Extract popup message text content for validation
Verify popup message matches expected 'Cancel this Shortcut?' text
Confirm popup displays appropriate action buttons (Cancel/Confirm)
Validate popup is properly displayed and interactive

automation_scripts_rules.applies_to: Test cases with implicit prerequisite steps marked as 'Required for Test Execution'
rule_definition: WHEN: A test step is identified as 'Implicit Step – Required for Test Execution' containing multiple setup actions
THEN: Break down the compound prerequisite into granular automation steps executed before main test function

IMPLEMENTATION: Execute all intermediary steps in sequence: Initialize test environment and device connection, Install HPX application on target test device, Launch HPX application and wait for home screen load, Locate printer icon element on root view screen using resource-id or content-desc locator, Execute click action on printer icon to navigate to device details, Identify shortcuts tile element on device details page, Perform click action on shortcuts tile to access shortcuts screen, Locate 'Add new shortcuts' option on shortcuts screen, Click on 'Add new shortcuts' to prepare for shortcut creation workflow

automation_scripts_rules.applies_to: Test steps with single atomic actions mapping to single script methods
rule_definition: WHEN: A test step describes a single user action that maps directly to one automation method
THEN: Implement the action using the mapped script line with complete intermediary step breakdown

IMPLEMENTATION: For 'Step 5: Then click on the Create your own shortcut arrow button' use 'self.hpx_shortcuts.click_create_your_own_shortcut()' with intermediary steps: Locate 'Create your own shortcut' arrow button element using resource-id or xpath locator, Verify button element is displayed and enabled for interaction, Execute click action on the arrow button element, Wait for navigation transition to Add Shortcut screen, Validate successful screen transition to shortcut creation interface

automation_scripts_rules.applies_to: Test steps where user actions trigger UI state changes requiring validation
rule_definition: WHEN: A test step involves clicking an element that should trigger a specific UI response or state change
THEN: Implement the action method with embedded validation or monitoring for the expected response

IMPLEMENTATION: For 'Step 6: Click on the back button in Add Shortcut screen' use 'self.hpx_shortcuts.click_cancel_shortcut_go_back_btn()' with intermediary steps: Identify back/cancel button element in Add Shortcut screen header, Verify button element is visible and clickable, Execute click action on back/cancel button, Monitor for UI state change or popup trigger, Capture action result for subsequent validation

automation_scripts_rules.applies_to: Test cases requiring validation of expected results that may be handled implicitly
rule_definition: WHEN: A test step is marked as 'Implicit Step – Supporting Step – Purpose of Test Case Validation' for verifying expected behavior
THEN: Ensure validation logic is either embedded in the triggering method or implemented as explicit assertion

IMPLEMENTATION: For verification steps like 'Step 7: Verify the pop up screen - Expected Result: Cancel this Shortcut?' pop up window should be displayed', implement intermediary steps: Wait for popup dialog element to appear after back button click, Locate popup container using dialog resource-id or alert locator, Extract popup message text content for validation, Verify popup message matches expected 'Cancel this Shortcut?' text, Confirm popup displays appropriate action buttons (Cancel/Confirm), Validate popup is properly displayed and interactive

automation_scripts_rules.description: Test Case Purpose Validation Enforcement Rule
automation_scripts_rules.applies_to: Test cases of type 'Unable to retrieve' or similar validation-focused scenarios
rule_definition: WHEN: Executing test cases that focus on validating specific application behaviors or error conditions
THEN: Always include supporting validation steps even if not explicitly mentioned in manual test steps

IMPLEMENTATION: For test cases like 'Unable to retrieve', ensure all implicit supporting steps for purpose validation are included, such as popup verification, error message validation, or state confirmation steps, because they are required to fulfill the purpose of the test case validation

automation_scripts_rules.applies_to: All test cases with multiple steps requiring specific execution sequence
rule_definition: WHEN: Test case contains multiple steps that must be executed in a specific order
THEN: Ensure all steps are implemented in the exact sequence defined, with proper dependencies and state validation between steps

IMPLEMENTATION: Execute prerequisite steps first, followed by action steps in numerical order, then validation steps, ensuring each step completes successfully before proceeding to the next, with appropriate wait conditions and error handling between steps

automation_scripts_rules.applies_to: All automation script methods requiring detailed implementation breakdown
rule_definition: WHEN: Implementing any automation script method
THEN: Break down the method into granular intermediary steps that are independently verifiable and follow atomic action principles

IMPLEMENTATION: Each intermediary step must include element identification strategy, action execution, wait conditions, and validation checkpoints, ensuring complete traceability from test step to implementation detail
